skill.name: outcome-extraction-for-clinical-trials
skill.description: Clinical research outcome extraction for meta-analysis. Use when users need to extract outcome measures (binary, continuous, or survival data) from clinical research papers for systematic review and meta-analysis. Handles both database lookup by PMID and real-time LLM extraction.
skill.summary: Extract structured outcome data from clinical research papers for systematic review and meta-analysis.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 48 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 13 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings were triggered by natural language text in JSON schemas, argparse descriptions, and YAML frontmatter - no actual cryptographic code exists. The 32 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings were triggered by Markdown fenced code blocks (triple backticks for prompt examples and JSON schemas), not executable Ruby code. The 4 'system reconnaissance' findings flagged audit commentary and usage instructions. The 2 'hardcoded URL' findings cite a GitHub source repository in documentation. The 1 'file write' finding identifies the core documented PDF text extraction function. The skill is a legitimate clinical research tool with no malicious intent, data exfiltration, or hidden behavior.

content.user_title: Extract Clinical Trial Outcomes for Meta-Analysis
content.value_statement: Clinical researchers spend hours manually extracting binary, continuous, and survival outcome data from research papers. This skill automates structured outcome extraction using LLM analysis and a PDF text extraction script, producing consistent JSON output for systematic review and meta-analysis workflows.
actual_capabilities: Extract binary outcome data (events and sample sizes) from clinical research papers
Extract continuous outcome data (means and standard deviations) across intervention groups
Extract survival outcome data (hazard ratios with confidence intervals)
Extract text from PDF research papers using a dedicated Python script
Structure extracted outcomes into consistent JSON format for meta-analysis tools
Look up existing results by PMID before performing new LLM extraction
limitations: Requires full paper text as input, not just abstract
Cannot access paywalled or subscription-only paper databases
Extracted data accuracy depends on paper clarity and reporting completeness
Does not perform statistical analysis or meta-analysis calculations

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Data Collection
use_cases.description: A medical researcher extracts binary and continuous outcome data from 30 clinical trial papers for a Cochrane systematic review on diabetes treatments.

use_cases.title: Meta-Analysis Data Preparation
use_cases.description: A graduate student in epidemiology extracts survival outcome data from oncology publications to prepare datasets for meta-analysis software.

use_cases.title: Clinical Data Validation
use_cases.description: A clinical data manager extracts and standardizes outcome reporting across multiple trial publications to verify consistency in submitted regulatory documents.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Binary Outcomes from Paper
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the outcome-extraction-for-clinical-trials skill. Extract binary outcomes from this clinical trial paper. Provide the full paper text below. I need the sample sizes and event counts for each intervention group for all binary outcome measures.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a clinical trial paper and needs binary outcome data (events and sample sizes)

prompt_templates.title: Extract Outcomes with PMID Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the outcome-extraction-for-clinical-trials skill. Look up PMID 12345678 and extract all continuous outcomes from this paper. If no database record exists, perform real-time LLM extraction from the full paper text.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a PMID and wants to check for pre-existing extracted data before new extraction

prompt_templates.title: Extract Mixed Outcome Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the outcome-extraction-for-clinical-trials skill. I need both binary and survival outcomes from this oncology trial paper. Extract all intervention groups and report data at the 12-month and 24-month time points. Format as structured JSON.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs multiple outcome types from the same paper with specific time points

prompt_templates.title: Extract PDF and Structure Outcomes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the outcome-extraction-for-clinical-trials skill. First run scripts/extract_pdf.py on the paper PDF file to extract the full text. Then analyze the extracted text and extract all binary, continuous, and survival outcomes. Output the results in the JSON format documented in references/extraction-prompts.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a PDF file and needs the full pipeline from text extraction to structured outcome data

output_examples.input: Extract binary outcomes from a randomized controlled trial comparing metformin and placebo for type 2 diabetes, with 200 patients in each group and 45 events in the metformin group and 68 events in the placebo group.
output_examples.output: Extracted binary outcome 'Glycemic Control' at 12-month detection time point. Metformin group: sample size 200, events 45. Placebo group: sample size 200, events 68.

output_examples.input: Extract continuous outcomes from a hypertension trial comparing drug A and drug B, with mean systolic blood pressure of 125 mmHg (SD 10) for drug A and 132 mmHg (SD 12) for drug B.
output_examples.output: Extracted continuous outcome 'Systolic Blood Pressure' at 6-month detection time point. Drug A group: sample size 150, mean 125, SD 10. Drug B group: sample size 148, mean 132, SD 12.

best_practices: Always provide the full paper text to ensure complete data extraction across all outcome types
Verify extracted numerical values against original paper tables before using in meta-analysis
Use specific intervention group names from the paper rather than generic labels for reproducibility
anti_patterns: Extracting data from abstracts only, which misses important details in the full results section
Skipping the validation step of comparing extracted values against original paper figures and tables
Using generic group labels like treatment and control instead of specific intervention names

faq.question: What types of outcome data can this skill extract?
faq.answer: The skill extracts binary outcomes (events and sample sizes), continuous outcomes (means and standard deviations), and survival outcomes (hazard ratios with confidence intervals).

faq.question: Do I need a PMID to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, a PMID is optional. If you provide one, the skill checks for pre-existing extracted data. If no PMID or no match, the skill performs real-time LLM extraction from the paper text.

faq.question: Can the skill extract data from any PDF research paper?
faq.answer: Yes, the scripts/extract_pdf.py tool can extract text from any PDF file. However, accuracy depends on the PDF quality, text layer availability, and table formatting in the original document.

faq.question: Does this skill perform meta-analysis calculations?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on outcome extraction and structured data preparation only. The extracted JSON output is designed for use with separate meta-analysis software.

faq.question: How does the skill handle missing or unreported data?
faq.answer: If data is not found for a specific outcome or group, the skill outputs a blank space for that field rather than omitting the entry entirely.

faq.question: What is the output format of the extracted data?
faq.answer: The output is a structured JSON array with outcome name, detection time point, intervention groups, and numeric data fields. Each entry includes sample size and type-specific values.
